summary.intent: Migrate from GitHub PAT to GitHub App credentials for Pipelines-as-Code authentication on OpenShift/Konflux cluster, with a cluster-side solution that automatically handles token generation when PipelineRuns start.
summary.outcome: Implemented a solution where doozer generates a fresh GitHub App installation token at the start of each invocation, creates a uniquely-named transient secret (art-transient-pipeline-auth-<nanosecond-timestamp>), uses it for all PipelineRuns in that batch, and cleans up stale secrets >24h old. PR #2711 opened with 194 passing tests.

repo: ART intentionally disables PAC because they manually trigger PipelineRuns via konflux_client.py rather than using webhooks
Git-auth secret is only consumed by clone-repository and prefetch-dependencies tasks (first few minutes), then all work uses OCI trusted artifacts - token expiry is not an issue for multi-hour builds
Codebase already uses time.time_ns() for generating collision-resistant unique IDs (e.g., doozer-watch-id)
Build flow has 4 distinct paths that all needed updating: image builds, FBC merge, FBC rebase-and-build, and OLM bundle builds
github_auth.py already has GitHub App token generation with 50-minute caching and multi-org support

code.finding: Uses time.time_ns() for _common_runtime_watcher_label_value to generate collision-resistant unique IDs
start_pipeline_run_for_image_build already accepts git_auth_secret parameter with default pipelines-as-code-secret
git-auth workspace only mounted to clone-repository (basic-auth) and prefetch-dependencies (git-basic-auth) tasks, not used by build/scan tasks

workflow: Pipelines-as-Code secret-auto-create feature only works for webhook-triggered PipelineRuns, not manually created ones
Creating unique per-invocation secrets with label-based cleanup avoids contention vs patching a shared secret with hundreds of concurrent operations
Best practice for idempotent cleanup: catch and ignore 404 errors when deleting resources that may already be deleted concurrently
Kubernetes V1Secret.data field expects base64-encoded values; stringData accepts plain text
Correct secret type for Tekton git-clone is kubernetes.io/basic-auth with username x-access-token for GitHub App tokens
Using nanosecond timestamps (time.time_ns) instead of second-level (time.time) effectively eliminates collision risks

friction: Initial confusion about whether to modify art-tools code vs deploy cluster-side infrastructure (CronJob approach)
PAC controller's secret-auto-create looked promising but doesn't work for manually-triggered PipelineRuns
Concern about 200+ concurrent builds patching same shared secret required rethinking approach
Pre-commit hook required sandbox escape for commit to succeed
Truncated diff output when reviewing all changes at once
Test failures with asyncio.get_event_loop() deprecation warnings in broader test suite
PR #2707 was closed externally and needed to be recreated as PR #2711
open_items: CodeRabbit review comments to address: wrap cleanup in finally blocks, remove self._git_auth_secret instance attribute, add label_selector assertion to tests
RBAC permissions required for doozer ServiceAccount to create/delete secrets not documented
No monitoring/alerting configured for secret creation/deletion failures (currently just logged warnings)
Assumes github-app-credentials secret exists on cluster with proper app-id and private-key.pem keys
